prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_FT_rf', seasonality_champion='THETA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds show mixed trend directions (one down, two up) with THETA dominating as overall champion and seasonality tracker. Early horizon specialists (THETA/ETS/ARIMA) differ significantly from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost/DWT_rf), indicating strong horizon specialization. With rmse_spread_ratio of 1.98 and seasonality_corr_variance of 0.808, models disagree substantially on both trend and seasonality patterns, making DBA combination the optimal approach to leverage complementary strengths across horizons.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_FT_rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "THETA",
  "overall_champion": "THETA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "THETA", "late": "DWT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["THETA", "ETS", "ARIMA", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "NaiveMovingAverage", "FT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "NaiveSeasonal"],
  "tier2_models": ["DWT_catboost", "catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "rf", "svr", "FT_catboost"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 1.9804,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds show mixed trend directions (one down, two up) with THETA dominating as overall champion and seasonality tracker. Early horizon specialists (THETA/ETS/ARIMA) differ significantly from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost/DWT_rf), indicating strong horizon specialization. With rmse_spread_ratio of 1.98 and seasonality_corr_variance of 0.808, models disagree substantially on both trend and seasonality patterns, making DBA combination the optimal approach to leverage complementary strengths across horizons."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 weighted (exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0, inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25). No leakage detected - all candidates use safe strategies. Universe leaderboards show ridge_stacking_l2100_topk5 (RMSE=4450.8) and best_single_by_validation (SMAPE=0.0814, MAPE=0.0842) but current set already has diverse types. No redundancy issues - candidates use different methods. Score margin between top2 is 0.015 which is acceptable. No changes needed.",

  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds show mixed trend directions (one down, two up) with THETA dominating as overall champion and seasonality tracker. Early horizon specialists (THETA/ETS/ARIMA) differ significantly from late horizon specialists (DWT_catboost/DWT_rf), indicating strong horizon specialization. With rmse_spread_ratio of 1.98 and seasonality_corr_variance of 0.808, models disagree substantially on both trend and seasonality patterns, making DBA combination the optimal approach to leverage complementary strengths across horizons.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=10 indicates strong horizon specialization requiring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread_ratio=1.98 (THETA=3185 vs ONLY_FT_svr=11756) justifies top-k averaging with small k=3 to avoid outlier sensitivity. n_windows=3 requires shrinkage≥0.25 for inverse_rmse_weights. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination due to seasonality_corr_variance=0.808 and horizon_heterogeneous=true, making DBA optimal for phase-shift robustness.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 weighted (exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0, inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25). No leakage detected - all candidates use safe strategies. Universe leaderboards show ridge_stacking_l2100_topk5 (RMSE=4450.8) and best_single_by_validation (SMAPE=0.0814, MAPE=0.0842) but current set already has diverse types. No redundancy issues - candidates use different methods. Score margin between top2 is 0.015 which is acceptable. No changes needed.
